title: Neoachmandra gilletii
intro: Neoachmandra gilletii, also known as Chondrodendron gilletii or Curare vine, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa, belonging to the Menispermaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including pain, inflammation, and fever, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Neoachmandra gilletii include alkaloids such as tubocurarine, which has been isolated and used in medicine for its muscle-relaxing properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and also used in the production of arrow poison, highlighting its historical use in hunting and medicine.
taxonomy: Neoachmandra gilletii is a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. It is a tropical plant native to the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Neoachmandra gilletii is within the kingdom Plantae, and it is a member of the order Malpighiales. The family Achariaceae is a small family of flowering plants, and Neoachmandra gilletii is one of its species. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
